---
title: "SL_final_proj"
author: "Michele Maslowski"
date: "2025-05-01"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

rm(list = ls()) #Clear workspace

library("tidyverse")          #For data manipulation
library("ggplot2")            #For nice plots
library('Metrics')
library('gridExtra')
library('dplyr')
library("ISLR")
library("glmnet")
library("MASS")
library("caret")
library("splines")
library("boot")
library("class")
library("nnet")
library('Rmpfr')
library('reshape2')
library('pROC')
library(e1071)  

```

```{r load data}

data <- read.csv("C:/Users/mmasl/OneDrive - Marquette University/Desktop/Statistical Learning/final_proj/train.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ",")
# converting catergorical variables
data$Sex <- factor(data$Sex)
data$Sex <- as.numeric(data$Sex)

data$Diet <- factor(data$Diet)
data$Diet <- as.numeric(data$Diet)

data$Country <- factor(data$Country)
data$Country <- as.numeric(data$Country)

data$Continent <- factor(data$Continent)
data$Continent <- as.numeric(data$Continent)

data$Hemisphere <- factor(data$Hemisphere)
data$Hemisphere <- as.numeric(data$Hemisphere)

# converting blood pressure to usable values
data <- data %>%
  separate(Blood.Pressure, into = c("systolic", "diastolic"), sep = "/") %>%
  mutate(across(c(systolic, diastolic), as.numeric))

# converting int to num
data <- data %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.integer), as.numeric))

# check for NaNs
NaNs = colSums(is.na(data))
# no missing values 

# remove sub ID 
clean_data <- subset(data, select = -Patient.ID)

```

```{r feature Correlation}

cor_matrix <- cor(clean_data, use = "complete.obs", method = "pearson")
cor_matrix_melted <- melt(cor_matrix)

# Plot the heatmap with correlation numbers using ggplot2
ggplot(cor_matrix_melted, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", high = "red", mid = "white", midpoint = 0) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, fill = "Correlation") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 2)), color = "black", size = 3)


# test train split 
train_indices <- sample(1:nrow(clean_data), size = 0.8 * nrow(clean_data))

# split
train_data <- clean_data[train_indices, ]
test_data <- clean_data[-train_indices, ]

test_label <- as.factor(test_data$Heart.Attack.Risk)
#test_data <- dplyr::select(test_data, -Heart.Attack.Risk)

```

```{r SVM (predicting heart attack (0 / 1))}
train_data$Heart.Attack.Risk <- factor(train_data$Heart.Attack.Risk, levels = c(0, 1))
test_data$Heart.Attack.Risk <- factor(test_data$Heart.Attack.Risk, levels = c(0, 1))

# Train the model
svm_model <- svm(Heart.Attack.Risk ~ ., data = train_data, kernel = "radial")

# Predict class labels (no "type = response")
svm_pred <- predict(svm_model, newdata = test_data)

# Confusion matrix
conf_matrix = confusionMatrix(svm_pred, test_data$Heart.Attack.Risk)

conf_matrix_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(conf_matrix))

conf_matrix_melt <- melt(conf_matrix_df, id.vars = c("Reference", "Prediction"))

ggplot(conf_matrix_melt, aes(x = Prediction, y = Reference, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = value), color = "black", size = 6) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix", x = "Predicted", y = "True") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

roc_obj <- roc(test_label, as.numeric(svm_pred))
plot(roc_obj, col = "blue", main = "ROC Curve for SVM")

precision <- conf_matrix$byClass["Precision"]
recall <- conf_matrix$byClass["Recall"]

f1_score <- 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)

```

```{r PCA }

pca_result <- prcomp(train_data[, -ncol(train_data)], center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
pc_scores <- data.frame(pca_result$x[, 1:5])
pc_scores$Heart.Attack.Risk <- train_data$Heart.Attack.Risk
ggplot(pc_scores, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Heart.Attack.Risk)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "PCA: First Two Principal Components",
       x = "Principal Component 1",
       y = "Principal Component 2") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("0" = "red", "1" = "blue"))

# finding the best component

explained_var <- pca_result$sdev^2 / sum(pca_result$sdev^2)
plot(explained_var, type = "b", xlab = "Principal Component", ylab = "Proportion of Variance Explained")
```
